title: Mandatory Returns and Unroutable Publication
description: A Go 1.26 service team must continuously consume return notifications and correlate them without assuming cross-channel event ordering; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. Learners cumulatively produce a correlated Go return and route-evidence ledger through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Register and drain return notifications before mandatory publication and correlate content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.
Distinguish broker confirms from consumer processing and combine mandatory returns or alternate routing with confirm results; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Build topology fixtures that assert expected destination sets, forbidden routes, unroutable cases, permissions, and changed bindings; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
